skill.name: creating-skills
skill.description: Guides users through creating, validating, and packaging Agent Skills with clear SKILL.md structure, focused resources, and practical review patterns.
skill.summary: Create effective Agent Skills with structured guidance, templates, validation scripts, and packaging steps.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: The static scan reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the command, eval, weak-crypto, and network matches are Markdown examples or ordinary prose. The skill does include Python scripts that create files, read SKILL.md, and build zip-based .skill archives, so publication is acceptable with a low filesystem warning.

medium_findings.title: Aggressive Activation Prompt Guidance
medium_findings.description: The activation reference recommends mandatory evaluation language and says aggressive words make instructions harder to ignore. This is not an active injection against the auditor, but it teaches prompt-control patterns that should be reviewed before broad marketplace distribution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly instructs use of mandatory and critical language to influence model behavior. It appears educational rather than malicious, so confidence is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Recursive Skill Packaging Writes Archive Files
low_findings.description: The packaging script uses zipfile to create a .skill archive from a user-supplied skill directory. This is intended behavior, but users should review packaged contents before sharing archives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The archive creation and recursive file inclusion are directly visible. The use is legitimate for packaging, so the residual risk is low.

low_findings.title: Skill Initializer Writes User-Selected Directories
low_findings.description: The initializer resolves a provided path, creates a skill directory, and writes template resource files. This filesystem activity is expected for a scaffolding tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly creates directories and writes files under the supplied path. It does not hide the operation or target sensitive paths by itself.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings occur in Markdown code fences, inline command examples, diagrams, or template text. No subprocess, shell, os.system, or backtick execution was found in executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation blocks or string templates, not runtime shell execution. The reviewed Python scripts do not import subprocess or invoke shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to skill metadata, ordinary documentation, regex validation, and template text. No weak hash, cipher, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operations in the cited areas. The apparent matches are scanner keyword collisions with unrelated prose and validation code.

low_findings.title: Static Network URL Match Is A Source Citation
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URL is a citation to an external article in reference documentation. The skill does not make network requests or send data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears under a Source heading and is not used by executable code. No network libraries or request calls were found in the reviewed scripts.

low_findings.title: Static eval Finding Is Prose, Not Dynamic Code Execution
low_findings.description: The eval finding refers to the phrase Forced eval in a documentation heading. No eval() call or dynamic code execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a Markdown label and code-fence instruction example, not executable code. There is no dynamic evaluation primitive at that location.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Match Is Name Validation
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance finding maps to a regex check for the skill name format and an error message. It does not inspect host details or enumerate system state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code only validates a name string from SKILL.md. It does not run discovery commands, read environment secrets, or collect machine information.

content.value_statement: Creating useful AI skills is hard when structure, triggers, and validation are unclear. This skill provides a practical workflow for drafting, checking, and packaging skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides users through planning scripts, references, and assets for a skill.
Provides an initializer script that scaffolds a new skill directory.
Provides a verifier script that checks frontmatter and trigger requirements.
Provides a packager script for creating distributable .skill archives.
Includes references for activation, progressive disclosure, and review patterns.
limitations: It does not guarantee that a generated skill is secure or high quality.
The packaging script expects quick_validate to be available in the Python path.
The activation guidance includes assertive prompt language that teams may want to soften.
Users must review generated scripts and assets before publishing a skill.

use_cases.title: Create a First Skill
use_cases.description: Use the workflow and initializer to scaffold a new skill with correct files and clear TODO sections.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skills
use_cases.description: Use the design guidance to keep team skills concise, focused, and consistent across projects.

use_cases.title: Prepare Marketplace Packages
use_cases.description: Run validation and packaging steps before sharing a skill with other users or teams.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Beginner Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a new skill for [task]. Ask for the examples you need, then propose the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my skill folder for trigger clarity, resource organization, and validation gaps. Recommend focused edits only.

prompt_templates.title: Add Reusable Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these repeated workflows, decide which scripts, references, or assets belong in the skill and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill for marketplace readiness. Check description triggers, validation results, packaging risks, and documentation quality.

output_examples.input: I want a skill that helps create customer support macros.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with trigger conditions, core workflow sections, and suggested reference files for tone rules and escalation policy.

output_examples.input: My skill has a long SKILL.md and several examples.
output: A recommendation to keep only the core workflow in SKILL.md.
A list of reference files to move detailed examples into.
Validation checks to run before packaging.

output_examples.input: Prepare this skill for sharing with my team.
output_examples.output: A readiness review covering frontmatter, scripts, references, assets, verification results, and packaging cautions.

best_practices: Keep the description focused on when the skill should trigger.
Move detailed variants into references instead of overloading SKILL.md.
Run verification before packaging or sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not use the description to summarize the full workflow.
Do not include extra documentation that does not support the skill task.
Do not publish generated scripts without reading and testing them.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Agent Skills with clear triggers, structured instructions, optional resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete production skill automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides scaffolding and guidance, but users must complete, review, and test the skill content.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that support skill-style instructions.

faq.question: Why does it include scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts support scaffolding, verification, and packaging tasks that are easier to perform deterministically.

faq.question: What should I review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review generated files, script behavior, trigger wording, bundled assets, and the final package contents.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests was found. The only URL reviewed is a documentation citation.
